entries.title: Homily on 1 Corinthians 44 on first-corinthians 16:23
entries.excerpt:
"The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you."

This is like a teacher, to help not only with advice, but also with prayers.

entries.title: COMMENTARY ON THE FIRST EPISTLE TO THE CORINTHIANS 285 on first-corinthians 16:23
entries.excerpt: It was Paul's usual custom to pray that the grace of Christ would be with those to whom he is writing.

entries.title: Commentary on 1 Corinthians on first-corinthians 16:23
entries.excerpt: The duty of a teacher is not only to persuade, but also to pray, and thus to confirm in a twofold manner: by teaching and by drawing down help from above through his prayers.

entries.title: Commentary on 1 Corinthians on first-corinthians 16:23
entries.excerpt: Finally, he blesses the good ones, wishing them well, namely, the grace of Christ, when he says: the grace of the Lord Jesus be with you. And wishing this, he wishes them every good, because in the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ is contained every good.
